title: Introduction
description: NOC 64201 – Image, social and other personal consultants | GTR Immigration In Canada’s increasingly image-conscious and experience-driven economy, NOC 64201 (Image, social and other personal consultants) professionals are the essential advisors for personal and professional branding. From fashion consultants and color analysts to etiquette experts and wedding planners, these TEER 4 specialists help individuals and corporate clients navigate social and professional landscapes. For immigration, this role offers a creative and high-flexibility pathway, particularly through Self-Employed or Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs) that value the personal services sector.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level4Major Group64 – Sales and service representativesSub-Major Group642 – Specialized sales and service occupationsMinor Group6420 – Specialized sales and service occupationsNOC Code64201

duties: Advise clients on wardrobe, hairstyle, makeup, and other aspects of physical appearance.
Plan and coordinate special events, such as weddings, anniversaries, and corporate galas.
Advise on social etiquette, speech, and public presentation skills.
Conduct color analysis and provide personalized shopping services.
Develop branding strategies for individuals or corporate teams.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of a college or other program in fashion, event planning, or a related field is often preferred.
Several years of experience in a related field, such as sales or hospitality, is typically necessary.
job_outlook: The outlook is Stable. Growth in this field is largely driven by individual entrepreneurship and the expansion of the "celebration economy" as more Canadians prioritize professional planning for life events.
labour_market_demand: The demand for personal consultants is rated as Moderate to Good, with significant growth in major urban centers like Toronto and Montreal. As social media and professional networking platforms increase the importance of "personal branding," there is a rising need for image and etiquette consultants. Wedding and event planners also see consistent demand due to Canada's robust $5 billion wedding industry.
Entrepreneurial Independence: High rates of self-employment and the ability to set your own consulting fees.
Diverse Markets: Opportunities to work with corporate executives, celebrities, and multicultural wedding markets.
Creative Fulfillment: Help clients transform their confidence and life milestones through your specialized expertise.
pathways: Self-Employed Persons Program: A key route for world-class planners or consultants with significant independent experience.
PNP Personal Services Streams: Selective opportunities in provinces with active service-sector recruitment.
CEC (Canadian Experience Class): Accessible for consultants employed by established firms for 12 months.
